COIN.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP Stagnate, But Dogecoin Rallies After Fed Leaves Rates Unchanged: Analyst Flags 'Insufficient Evidence' To Suggest Bull Cycle

macro_events.event_summary: Brent crude surges above $121/bbl on reports US military will brief Trump on potential action against Iran, raising risk of Middle East conflict and oil supply disruption.
primary_drivers: Geopolitical risk premium on crude with potential Strait of Hormuz disruption (~20% of global oil flows)
Higher realized prices for upstream producers and refining crack spreads
Defense spending tailwind and safe-haven flows; airline/transport fuel cost shock

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with large upstream exposure; every $10/bbl move in Brent adds materially to FCF. Direct leverage to higher realized crude prices.
Highly leveraged Permian pure-play producer with above-average oil mix and balance sheet leverage; Brent at $121 drives outsized FCF and equity beta to crude.
Permian pure-play E&P with high oil cut and low breakevens; direct beneficiary of sustained Brent above $100 via cash returns/buybacks.
Prime contractor for THAAD, PAC-3 Patriot missiles, and F-35 — direct beneficiaries of any Iran kinetic action and likely supplemental defense appropriations and allied restocking.
Maker of Patriot, Tomahawk, and SM-series interceptors heavily used in Middle East theater; missile inventory drawdowns drive replenishment orders.
B-2/B-21 stealth bomber and Global Hawk ISR provider — directly relevant assets if strikes on hardened Iranian nuclear sites occur.
Offshore oil services vessel operator with global fleet; sustained high crude pulls forward offshore activity and dayrate momentum, plus Middle East exposure premium.
Largest US independent refiner; geopolitical disruption typically widens crack spreads as product prices rise faster than crude inputs, especially distillates.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of operating costs and Delta does not hedge; Brent at $121 directly compresses margins with limited near-term ability to pass through via fares.
Unhedged fuel exposure; international long-haul routes exposed to both fuel cost and potential Middle East airspace closures/rerouting costs.
Highest financial leverage among legacy carriers and unhedged on fuel — most vulnerable to sustained crude shock on both EPS and balance sheet.
Cruise operator with massive bunker fuel exposure and discretionary consumer demand; double hit from fuel costs and potential Middle East itinerary cancellations.
Express air freight network with heavy jet fuel burn; fuel surcharges lag spot prices, creating margin compression in the interim.
Commodity chemicals producer with naphtha/ethane feedstocks linked to crude; energy-intensive operations face margin squeeze if pass-through lags.
Mass retailer where rising gasoline prices act as a regressive tax on core low-income consumer, redirecting wallet share away from discretionary categories.

headlines: Brent crude tops $121 as U.S. military to reportedly brief Trump on potential action against Iran
